text.title: MCP Gateway — Three Composition Patterns
text.summary: Pick virtual server for tool curation, federation for tree-structured org topology, per-client visibility for role-based filtering.

section.title: The three patterns

rule.statement: When you put an MCP gateway in front of multiple servers, pick exactly one composition pattern per agent: (a) virtual server — gateway exposes a curated subset of upstream tools as one logical server; (b) federation — gateways consume other gateways in a tree (used for org-wide rollouts); (c) per-client visibility — gateway filters the tool list by user/role/scope. Mixing patterns inside one gateway instance creates tool-list cycles and ambiguous deny rules.
rule.rationale: Each pattern has a different failure mode if applied to the wrong shape: virtual server breaks for hierarchical orgs (no nesting), federation breaks for end-user filtering (no per-call identity), per-client visibility breaks for static catalogs (overhead with no benefit). One pattern per gateway keeps deny rules unambiguous.

section.reference: Lunar — best open-source MCP gateways in 2026.

list.item: Virtual server: bundle GitHub + Linear + Sentry as `dev_workspace`; agent sees one server, ~12 curated tools.
Federation: org-level gateway federates per-team gateways; team additions do not require central reconfig.
Per-client visibility: same gateway exposes `read_*` to support agents and `write_*` only to engineers.

example.code: # Virtual server — one logical surface, curated tools, deny destructive verbs virtual_servers: - name: dev_workspace expose: - github.create_issue - github.search_issues - linear.create_ticket - sentry.search_events deny: ["github.delete_*", "linear.archive_*"]
example.note: Agent sees 4 tools, namespaced; gateway enforces deny list before the call hits upstream.

example.code: # Anti-shape: passthrough proxy (no curation, no deny rules, no namespacing) proxy: upstreams: [github, linear, sentry, jira, gitlab, slack, notion] expose: "*"
example.note: Tool list explodes (60+ schemas), namespace collisions on `search`, no audit trail. Just push servers directly to the agent and skip the broken gateway.

section.title: Failure modes

antipattern.description: Mixing virtual-server curation with per-client visibility in one config block (filter rules apply to a tool that has been renamed by curation).
antipattern.reason: Deny rules become ambiguous — the renamed tool may match neither the original nor the new name, silently leaking access.

antipattern.description: Federation gateway that re-exposes downstream tools without re-namespacing.
antipattern.reason: Two team gateways both named `search` collide at the org level. Agent picks at random.

section.reference: Reference virtual-server config with curation + deny.
